learningObjective: Spark curiosity about the tesseract by showing an animated rotating 4D hypercube, inviting students to wonder how it is constructed
ahaMoment: The student sees the mysterious rotating shape and becomes curious about how such a thing could be built

miniSpec.title: Animated Tesseract Hook
miniSpec.narrative_wrapper: A slowly rotating tesseract captures attention and sparks curiosity before the lesson begins
miniSpec.manipulated_variable: None - this is a passive animation to hook interest
miniSpec.primary_visual_feedback: The tesseract rotates continuously in 4D, showing its 3D shadow morphing
miniSpec.causal_chain_exposed: The visual complexity of the tesseract raises the question: how is this built?
miniSpec.aha_moment: Curiosity is sparked - the student wants to understand how this shape is constructed

assertions.description: On load, the animated tesseract is visible and rotating, posing the implicit question of how it is built
assertions.visualCheck: Tesseract is visible and animating smoothly with 16 vertices and 32 edges in a cube-within-cube projection
